title: This charge rests on forgery.

source.title: NPNF2-04. Athanasius: Select Works and Letters

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: It is this that has thrown the Churches everywhere into such confusion; for pretences have been devised, and Bishops of great authority, and of advanced age, have been banished for holding communion with me.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: And if matters had stopped here, our prospect would be favourable through your gracious interposition.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: But that the evil may not extend itself, let Truth prevail before you; and leave not every Church under suspicion, as though Christian men, nay even Bishops, could be guilty of plotting and writing in this manner.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: Or if you are unwilling to investigate the matter, it is but right that we who offer our defence, should be believed, rather than our calumniators.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: They, like enemies, are occupied in wickedness; we, as earnestly contending for our cause, present to you our proofs.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: And truly I wonder how it comes to pass, that while we address you with fear and reverence, they are possessed of such an impudent spirit, that they dare even to lie before the Emperor.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: But I pray you, for the Truth’s sake, and as it is written, ‘search diligently’ in my presence, on what grounds they affirm these things, and whence these letters were obtained.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: But neither will any of my servants be proved guilty, nor will any of his people be able to tell whence they came; for they are forgeries.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: And perhaps one had better not enquire further.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: They do not wish it, lest the writer of the letters should be certain of detection.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: For the calumniators alone, and none besides, know who he is.
